The EU has watered down its latest round of sanctions on Moscow after concluding that banning Greek companies from transporting Russian gas to third countries could hand strategically important shipping assets to China.
To remove a Greek veto against the 21st package of measures aimed at hobbling Russia’s war machine, the bloc’s member states agreed to allow EU operators to continue transporting Russian liquefied natural gas (LNG) to third countries for one year.

articles.title: Man held as mainland Chinese dental centre in Hong Kong raided amid promotions
articles.summary:
Hong Kong police have arrested a 72-year-old local man for allegedly practising dentistry illegally at a shop used by a mainland Chinese dental clinic to promote its business in the city, following calls from the local industry for tighter regulatory oversight of such activities.
The arrest was made during a joint operation conducted by police and the Department of Health on Thursday at a shop on 37 King’s Road in North Point.
The shop had been used to advertise a mainland clinic’s services,...

articles.title: Screen legend Patrick Tse saved my brother from drowning, woman recalls
articles.summary:
A Hong Kong woman has paid tribute to the late screen legend Patrick Tse Yin, revealing that he saved her brother from drowning decades ago on a boat trip.
Veteran actor, director and producer Tse, nicknamed “Brother Four”, died on July 16 at the age of 89, marking the loss of one of the last remaining icons from the golden era of local cinema, when the city’s film industry held sway across Southeast Asia.

articles.title: Investors ‘bulk buying’ flats remains key in Hong Kong amid red-hot rental market
articles.summary:
Hong Kong’s bulk homebuyers are expected to remain a major force in the city’s new-home market in the second half of the year, after investor purchases hit record levels in the first six months on strong rental demand.
A total of 654 buyers purchased two or more units in the primary market between January and June, acquiring 1,794 flats worth HK$17.4 billion (US$2.2 billion), according to Centaline Property.
The figures more than doubled from a year earlier and marked record highs in terms of...

articles.title: How China and Indonesia can build a truly sturdy partnership
articles.summary:
In April 1955, amid the Cold War, 304 representatives across 29 Asian and African nations convened in Bandung, Indonesia, to discuss the principles of a more complex future world order. Among them were India, China and host Indonesia.
While India would go on to champion the non-aligned movement at the 1961 Belgrade Conference, Mao Zedong’s China and Sukarno’s Indonesia were more sceptical of the colonial, Western order. Then the more US-sympathetic Suharto seized power in Indonesia in 1966....

articles.title: Despite regulatory crackdown, Chinese investors look to Hong Kong, US to diversify
articles.summary:
Around half of Chinese investors with assets over 1 million yuan (US$147,703) plan to increase their investments in Hong Kong and the United States over the next 12 months to diversify their portfolios, despite Beijing’s crackdown on cross-border brokerages, according to a new survey.
The findings come after the DBS Treasures Affluent Investor Survey polled 1,617 individuals in Hong Kong and mainland China with at least HK$1 million (US$127,525) or 1 million yuan in assets in their respective...

articles.title: China targets panda bond reform, mandates global credit mapping to lure foreign capital

articles.title: SEC files criminal complaint over Bitkub's alleged concealment of B1.7bn hack

articles.title: China’s durian imports from Thailand and Malaysia soar as glut pressures growers
articles.summary: HONG KONG — China's durian imports rose 47%, year on year, in the first half of 2026, with Thailand and Malaysia posting particularly strong gains, according to customs data, as Southeast Asian exporters offloaded a glut of the fruit that had sparked a price drop.

articles.title: Google hit with $1 billion fine for abusing its power in Europe

articles.title: Greece to build ‘Achilles Shield’ defence dome with Israeli arms
articles.summary:
Greece on Thursday approved the multibillion-dollar purchase of Israeli anti-missile, anti-aircraft and anti-drone weaponry to set up an “Achilles Shield” protective dome, a defence ministry source said.
Around €3 billion of a broader €4.2 billion (US$4.8 billion) spending spree will go towards Israeli-made systems for the new shield, the source said, speaking on condition of anonymity.
“Greece has entered a new era, a new reality,” Defence Minister Nikos Dendias said on social media, confirming...

articles.title: Hundreds hospitalised in Japan heatwave ‘disaster’
articles.summary: TOKYO - More than 450 people received hospital treatment for heat-related issues in a single day in Tokyo — the highest number since records began 16 years ago, emergency services said on Thursday.

articles.title: Iran vows to continue strikes in spreading Mideast war
articles.summary: TEHRAN - Iran vowed on Thursday to keep striking the region so long as it remains under attack, as the Middle East war spread to new fronts with Tehran’s Houthi allies attacking Saudi shipping in the Red Sea.

articles.title: Thousands flee wildfires in France, Italy and Spain
articles.summary: ROME - Wildfires continued to sweep across swathes of Italy, France and Spain on Thursday, forcing thousands to flee from blazes fanned by scorching temperatures and strong winds.

articles.title: 1.5 million speed pills seized in Nakhon Si Thammarat
articles.summary: NAKHON SI THAMMARAT – A suspect has been arrested with 1.5 million speed pills, firearms, a million baht in cash and other assets seized in Thung Song district of this southern province, authorities said on Thursday.

articles.title: Bitcoin miners ‘stole power worth B280 million’
articles.summary: The Department of Special Investigation has seized about 1,900 cryptocurrency mining machines after uncovering large-scale electricity theft estimated at 280 million baht linked to illegal bitcoin mining in Samut Sakhon.

articles.title: Man dies after knife attack at German bank
articles.summary:
A man died following a knife attack at a bank in the German city of Regensburg, a police spokeswoman told dpa on Thursday, adding that the suspected attacker remains inside the building with other people.
Special forces have arrived at the scene and the area around the branch has been cordoned off.
Initial reports suggested the perpetrator was holding people hostage inside the bank branch, but this later became unclear. The spokeswoman said an investigation was under way into homicide and...

articles.title: Man linked to UK synagogue attacker jailed over terror plan
articles.summary:
A friend of a man who went on a deadly stabbing spree at a Manchester synagogue was jailed for life by a UK court on Thursday for planning a separate terror attack with him.
Jihad Al-Shamie went on a knife rampage targeting a mosque in the northwestern city last October that killed two people. Al-Shamie was later shot dead by police.
His friend Mohammad Bashir, 31, last month pleaded guilty to the preparation of terrorist acts.
Last August, he drove Al-Shamie on a trip to spy on the UK’s Defence...

articles.title: Trump’s manufacturing push meets reality as costs rise and investment stalls
articles.summary:
At Zion Foodtrucks in the US state of Colorado, American-made steel and aluminium are combined with globally sourced stoves, refrigerators, vents and titanium piping to transform everyday vehicles into mobile restaurants.
But instead of benefiting from Donald Trump’s push to revive US manufacturing, the company says it has been squeezed by higher costs and tougher competition.
Since taking office, the US president’s import taxes on steel and aluminium have hit 50 per cent tied to his “beautiful”...

articles.title: 1 dead, 4 injured in Czech army helicopter crash
articles.summary:
One soldier died and four were injured on Thursday when a military helicopter crashed in the Czech Republic, the army said.
“A Venom helicopter has crashed at the 22nd helicopter base in Namest nad Oslavou. Five soldiers were on board. Emergency services are currently deployed at the scene,” the army wrote on social media.
In a later post, it added that one soldier was killed and four injured in the crash.
“Our absolute priority now is to provide all possible care and support to the injured...

articles.title: Can science bridge the US-China trust gap? Maybe with new rules, researchers say
articles.summary:
Scientific ties can be a stabilising force in relations between China and the US but as the two countries jostle for a hi-tech edge, maintaining these links will mean going beyond simple competition versus cooperation.
To get there, Beijing and Washington will need to keep in continuous contact and have clearly defined boundaries, with narrower areas of collaboration.
That was the finding Zhao Minghao, a professor at Fudan University’s Institute of International Studies, and Chen Yutong, a...

articles.title: Fields Medal win: Wang Hong, Deng Yu are first Chinese nationals to score top maths honour
articles.summary:
For the first time, China has produced Fields Medal winners educated on its own soil – and two at once.
Chinese mathematicians Wang Hong and Deng Yu took the honour, alongside John Pardon and Jacob Tsimerman.
The Fields Medal is the top honour for young mathematicians under 40 years of age. The medals, along with a modest cash prize, were given out on Thursday at the International Congress of Mathematicians (ICM) in Philadelphia.
The prestigious medal, often called the “Nobel Prize of...

articles.title: Hong Kong high-speed rail link sets record as passenger trips top 16m in 6 months
articles.summary:
Hong Kong’s cross-border high-speed rail link carried more than 16 million passengers in the first six months of the year – the highest since its debut in 2018 – prompting the introduction of new facilities to meet demand.
The MTR Corporation, which operates the 26km Guangzhou-Shenzhen-Hong Kong Express Rail Link, said on Thursday that patronage rose by more than 10 per cent year on year to over 16 million in the first six months, with average daily trips exceeding 92,000.
The company said...

articles.title: Shanghai to launch ‘choke point’ tech funds, relax pre-profit listing rules in key sectors
articles.summary:
Shanghai is exploring dedicated funds to tackle “choke point” technologies and new channels for offshore yuan capital to invest in China’s tech board, while allowing pre-profit tech firms in AI and nuclear fusion to go public.
The municipal government will encourage the launch of dedicated funds focused on addressing “supply-chain gaps and breaking through ‘choke point’ technologies”.
The funds would pool investment from corporate investment arms of state-owned enterprises, government-backed...

articles.title: Hong Kong’s competition watchdog tightens tender clauses to target bid-rigging
articles.summary:
Hong Kong’s competition watchdog has revised its tender clauses to require bidders to make a statutory declaration that they have not engaged in collusion on a project, as part of a wider move to criminalise bid-rigging following the city’s deadliest fire in decades.
Senior counsel Jat Sew-tong, chairman of the Competition Commission, also said on Thursday the body was considering proposing to raise the maximum penalty for the proposed criminal offence from seven to 10 years.
Tendering practices...
